pubmed_links: [Ravel et al. (2021) Bi-allelic SARS1 variant in children with neurodevelopmental delay, deafness, and cardiomyopathy](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34570399/);[Bögershausen et al. (2022) WARS1 and SARS1: Two tRNA synthetases implicated in autosomal recessive microcephaly](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35790048/);[Verdura et al. (2022) Loss of seryl-tRNA synthetase (SARS1) causes complex spastic paraplegia and cellular senescence](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36041817/)
summary: SARS1 encodes seryl-tRNA synthetase 1, and biallelic loss-of-function variants have been linked to autosomal recessive neurodevelopmental disorders including microcephaly, intellectual disability, developmental delay, deafness, and complex spastic paraplegia. These findings establish SARS1 as a cause of rare Mendelian neurodevelopmental disease, with aminoacyl-tRNA synthetase dysfunction emerging as a shared mechanism in neurological disorders. (emerging evidence)
